title: RNA TRIzol Isolation of Datura spp.
author: Eunice Kariñho-Betancourt
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bx4zpqx6
materials: Reagents Chloroform:Isoamyl alcohol (24:1) 100m l 96 ml Chloroform 4 ml Isoamyl alcohol Saline solution 500 ml 0.8M Sodium Citrate 1.2 M NaCl Distilled water 75% ethanol 100 ml 75 ml of ethanol molecular biology grade (100%) 25 ml of Distilled water )" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Isopropyl alcohol (Isopropanol) molecular biology grade TRIzol reagent (Invitrogen/Thermofisher) DNAsa I RNase free water

The integrity of each sample of RNA was first visualized by electrophoresis in 1.0% agarose stained with Ethidium Bromide (EtBr). The gel was run in TBE 0.5X buffer prepared in water treated with DEPC. Then the quantity and quality of RNA samples were verified by UV absorbance in Nanodrop (Thermo Scientific) and by fluorescence measurements with Qubit 3.0 (Invitrogen) using 2ul of each sample of RNA .

steps.title: Protocol Grind tissue to a powder...
steps.instruction: Protocol Grind tissue to a powder in liquid nitrogen with cold (-80°C 1 hr sterilized) mortar and pestle

steps.title: In a 1
steps.instruction: In a 1.5 ml Eppendorf tube add 50-100 mg of ground tissue plus 1ml of TRIzol Reagent and 5 μl of DNAsa I. Mix by vortex 30 seconds.

steps.title: Add two sterilized steel beads and...
steps.instruction: Add two sterilized steel beads and high-speed shake the tube in a Tissuelyser II (QIAGEN), at 30 Hz for 45 seconds. Repeat 2 more times

steps.title: Take out the beads and incubate...
steps.instruction: Take out the beads and incubate the mixture at room temperature in the same Eppendorf tube for 5 min.

steps.title: Centrifuge the tube at 13,000 rpm...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the tube at 13,000 rpm for 15 min at 4°C. All of the insoluble matter should form a pellet at the bottom of the tube.

steps.title: Transfer the aqueous phase (upper) in a new 1
steps.instruction: Transfer the aqueous phase (upper) in a new 1.5 ml tube and add 250 μl of isoamyl alcohol. Mix by inversion.

steps.title: Centrifuge the tube at 13,000 rpm...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the tube at 13,000 rpm for 15 min at 4°C.

steps.title: Transfer the upper aqueous phase in a new 1
steps.instruction: Transfer the upper aqueous phase in a new 1.5 ml tube and add 250 μl of Saline solution and then 250 μl of isopropanol.

steps.title: Mix by inversion and incubate on ice for 10 min
steps.instruction: Mix by inversion and incubate on ice for 10 min.

steps.title: Centrifuge )" target="_blank" rel="noopener">at 13,000 rpm...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge )" target="_blank" rel="noopener">at 13,000 rpm for 10 min at 4°C , and decant the supernatant, taking care not to lose the pellet.

steps.title: Add 1 ml 75% cooled ethanol...
steps.instruction: Add 1 ml 75% cooled ethanol to the pellet and centrifuge at 13,000 rpm for 10 min at 4°C and decant the supernatant

steps.title: Open the cap and air-dry the pellet
steps.instruction: Open the cap and air-dry the pellet.

steps.title: Add 30μl RNase-free water and heat...
steps.instruction: Add 30μl RNase-free water and heat at 55°C to resuspend the pellet carefully.

steps.title: Analyze (quantify concentration, quality, and integrity...
steps.instruction: Analyze (quantify concentration, quality, and integrity of the RNA) and store the tube at -80°C.
